cards.back: A protocol where two processes or threads can only access a shared resource one at a time
The process of synchronizing the actions of multiple processes or threads to achieve a common goal
A distributed computing system where a single token is passed between nodes to control access to a shared resource
A classic problem in distributed systems where two processes, one for toast and one for jam, cannot both access a toaster at the same time
A protocol used in distributed systems to synchronize access to shared resources, similar to a token ring
